rulings.Case Title: ELROY PORCHIA et al VS. 3M COMPANY, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN INTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 12, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Hobart Brothers Company's motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS LEO DOUGLAS TREMBLAY VS. ADERHOLT SPECIALTY COMPANY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 12, 2014 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to plaintiffs' causes of action for false representation and intentional tort and continued as to plaintiffs' causes of action for negligence and strict liability and claim for punitive damages. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense applies to bar plaintiffs' claims against it. Further, even if defendant had demonstrated that the defense applies, it is not a complete defense to any of plaintiffs' causes of action but only precludes claims based on failure to warn. Summary adjudication of plaintiffs' causes of action for false representation and intentional tort is granted because plaintiffs state that they waive these causes of action against defendant. The hearing on defendant's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' causes of action for negligence and strict liability and claim for punitive damages will be continued pursuant to plaintiffs' C.C.P. § 437c(h) request, which is granted. Plaintiffs demonstrated that facts essential to justify opposition of this issues may exist and explained why they could not already be presented. The parties must appear at 9:45a.m. to discuss supplemental briefing and a new hearing date. If a hearing is requested to contest the Court's substantive tentative ruling and/or its decision to grant plaintiffs' continuance request, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS LEO DOUGLAS TREMBLAY VS. ADERHOLT SPECIALTY COMPANY INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 12, 2014 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Honeywell International Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present clear and convincing evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs' objections to defendant's Exhibits 9, 10, 11 and 12 are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES OWEN et al VS. ASBESTOS DFDTS.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Cahill Construction Co., Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint For Survival, Wrongful Death - Asbestos
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 12, 2014 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Cahill Construction Co., Inc.'s motion to strike portions of Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint for survival and wrongful death is denied. The Court grants defendant's request for judicial notice, Exhibits A and B. Plaintiffs' complaint alleges facts sufficient to support a claim for punitive damages pursuant to C.C.P. § 3294. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
